suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the scrutiny validator/reviewer procedure so Task-spawned `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` subagents can run with automatic execution approval in this worker environment, or document the manual-review fallback explicitly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: In this platform-expansion scrutiny run, both Task invocations for `expand-platform-registry` and `new-platform-icons-and-app-icon` exited with `Exec ended early: insufficient permission to proceed`, so the validator had to review the transcript skeletons, handoffs, commit diffs, and current source manually instead of using the required reviewer subagents.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the platform registry guidance to include `copilot` and `aider` in the enumerated built-in platform list now that the codebase seeds them as built-in agents.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: `expand-platform-registry` commit `058dc5b` and `src-tauri/src/db.rs` now seed 28 built-ins including `copilot` and `aider`, but the mission AGENTS platform registry still lists `claude, codex, cursor, gemini, trae, trae-cn, factory, openclaw, qclaw, easyclaw, easyclaw-v2, workbuddy, autoclaw, junie, qwen, windsurf, qoder, augment, opencode, kilocode, ob1, amp, kiro, codebuddy, hermes, central` and omits those two supported IDs.
